transcript: That's quite a lamb you have there, sir.
Only trouble is, it's black. Uhh, kinda puts your lamb in a class by himself.
[singing] Oh, get out the way, oh lamb patch
Now, I'm not trying to be funny, folks, this lamb is in a class by himself. It's what you do with what you got that counts. This little lamb is a champion in every sense of the word.
Therefore, it is the unanimous decision of your judgin' committee, that there be awarded the breeder a special prize.
A special blue ribbon of merit for Jeremiah Kincaid. Congratulations, Mr. Kincaid!
Heh. Well, you've gotta rear 'em, haven't you, you ever think of that? That's a little ol' lamb's that weren't even born yet. But suddenly I heard a sound from over in the far corner of the pen.
Something seemed to be moving in the shadows over there.
There was another baby lamb! And he wasn't quite like lambs were supposed to be, he was black all over.
Oh, well, I know the nature of these black sheep.
And I've traded in my dream of a champion racehorse for an ol' [unintelligible] black lamb.
But I had a funny feeling that something good was going to come of it. That little black lamb had happened along just at that particular time, for a reason. What could we do now?
Well! [chuckles] Well now, if this ain't a fine howdy-do.
